ion: noun. An atom or group of atoms bearing an electrical charge, such as the sodium and chlorine atoms in a salt solution.
ism: noun. An ideology, system of thought, or practice that can be described by a word ending in -ism.
miosis: noun. Contraction of the pupil of the eye.
mis: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for unencoded languages.
miso: noun. A thick paste made by fermentation of soybeans with the mold Aspergillus oryzae, used in making soups and sauces.
miss: verb. To fail to hit, catch, grasp, etc.
mission: noun. A set of tasks that fulfills a purpose or duty; an assignment set by an employer, or by oneself.
mon: noun. The former currency of Japan until 1870, before the yen.
mono: noun. A bicycle or motorcycle trick where the front wheel is lifted off the ground while riding.
mons: noun. One of the fleshy areas at the base of the fingers; a mount.
moo: noun. The characteristic lowing sound made by cattle.
moon: noun. Any natural satellite of a planet.
mos: noun. singular of mores (“moral norms or customs”)
moss: noun. Any of various small, green, seedless plants growing on the ground or on the surfaces of trees, stones, etc.; now specifically, a
nims: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of nim
nomos: noun. The body of law, especially that governing human behaviour.
ons: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of on
sin: noun. A violation of divine will or religious law.
soon: adjective. Short in length of time from the present.
imi: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Anamgura.
imo: prepositional phrase. Alternative form of IMO.
isis: noun. Any coral of the genus Isis, or family Isididae, composed of joints of white, stony coral, alternating with flexible, horny joints.
isn: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Isanzu.
mino: noun. A traditional Japanese raincoat made from straw.
moio: noun. A traditional Portuguese dry measure, equal to about 0.78 – 1.15 m³ at different places and times.
nis: noun. A nix; a hobgoblin, especially one that resides in a farm house.
nosism: noun. A form of egotism in a group of people.
omni: noun. A person or animal who is not vegan; one with no particular dietary restrictions.
oni: noun. A Japanese evil spirit or demon.
oom: noun. An older man, especially an uncle. (Frequently as a respectful form of address.)
osi: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Osing.
simi: noun. A short sword or dagger used by the Kikuyu people.
